LR_DECAY_EPOCHS = [60, 80]","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"build_scheduler","new":"","old":"","op":"replace_function","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else max(1, steps_per_epoch * 100)
    warmup_steps = max(1, total_steps // 20)
    cosine_steps = max(1, total_steps - warmup_steps)
    warmup = optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(optimizer, start_factor=0.2, total_iters=warmup_steps)
    cosine = optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=cosine_steps, eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.05)
    if warmup_steps >= total_steps:
        return cosine
    return optim.lr_scheduler.SequentialLR(
        optimizer,
        schedulers=[warmup, cosine],
        milestones=[warmup_steps],
    )","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"SEED = 9408","old":"SEED = 42","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Use the fixed-step budget more effectively with a warmup-plus-cosine schedule, and pair it with a slightly larger AdamW CNN plus modest regularization.","secondary_modes":["layout","indexing","topk"]}

    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else max(1, steps_per_epoch * 100)
    return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(
        optimizer,
        T_max=total_steps,
        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.02,
    )"}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The compact AdamW + cosine setup is now working; the next low-risk move is to spend the fixed step budget more aggressively by increasing the learning rate and batch size together. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The current setup is stable and the best recent gains came from increasing the step size. {"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The learning-rate scaling sweep is working, and the remaining gap is small. Tightening the cosine tail should let the model settle harder in the last part of the fixed 256-step budget without disturbing the successful peak learning rate.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.02,","new":"        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.005,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The run is near the current ceiling, so the safest remaining probe is to let the cosine schedule anneal all the way to zero instead of stopping at a small floor. That preserves the successful learning-rate shape while squeezing a little more final settling out of the fixed step budget.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.005,","new":"        eta_min=0.0,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The optimizer and schedule are already tuned well enough that the remaining gains are likely to come from generalization rather than more step-size work. Mild label smoothing is a low-cost way to tighten validation loss on CIFAR-10 without changing the training budget or model shape.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"Label smoothing degraded validation loss in the last step, so the best next move is to remove it and return to the previously stronger loss objective. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The stronger smoothing value was too much, but a very small amount may still buy a bit of generalization without changing the rest of the tuned setup. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"The small label-smoothing probe hurt, so restore the stronger plain cross-entropy objective and keep moving along the learning-rate direction that had been steadily improving the run. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"The 3e-3 step was too aggressive, so return to the better 2.75e-3 scale and test a lighter decay penalty on top of it. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The last gain came from lighter decay at the same learning-rate scale, so the remaining margin is most likely in that same direction. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The recent gains have all come from backing off regularization, so the next clean probe is to remove weight decay entirely and see whether the compact model is still being held back. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Zero decay overshot the regularization optimum, but the earlier trend suggests the sweet spot is still a very small nonzero value. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The best gain came from increasing the batch size once, so a modest further increase is the most plausible remaining way to improve sample throughput without changing the tuned optimizer shape.
